raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I was charged {$3200.00} by XXXX for a short-term rental stay scheduled for XXXX XXXX XXXX  ( Reservation ID XXXX ). 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I checked into the property. The listing advertised approximately XXXX XXXX XXXX, a pool, and working XXXX. Upon arrival, the unit was approximately XXXX XXXX XXXX there was no functioning pool, and XXXX XXXX not work. 

Between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted XXXX XXXX  multiple times to report the misrepresentation and lack of amenities. XXXX agents opened multiple cases, confirmed escalation, and informed me that I qualified for a refund under XXXX XXXX with XXXX XXXX. I was told that a refund request had been submitted on my behalf. Relying on those assurances, I remained at the property instead of relocating. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I filed a dispute with Citibank for the full amount of {$3200.00} as services not as described. Citibank issued conditional credit. 

In XX/XX/XXXX, Citibank reversed the conditional credit after accepting the merchants response, without addressing the documented misrepresentation, the refund assurances provided by XXXX, or the fact that the listing was later XXXX. 

I am requesting that Citibank reopen the dispute, conduct a proper review consistent with card network rules for misrepresentation, and reinstate the disputed credit. 

Attached is written confirmation from XXXX that the host failed to provide sufficient evidence and that the listing was suspended as a result.
